description: Add task-mgr decisions subcommand for managing key decisions (list, resolve, decline, revert) and a /tm-decisions skill

criteria: Rust: No warnings from cargo check or cargo clippy
Rust: All tests pass with cargo test
No breaking changes to existing DB functions — extend, don't modify signatures
JSON output (--format json) works correctly for all new commands

priorityPhilosophy.description: Bottom-up: DB layer → CLI definition → command logic → wiring → skill
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Read all files before modifying; understand existing patterns
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Working end-to-end flow matters more than polish
3. CORRECTNESS - Compiles, type-checks, all tests pass
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Follow existing patterns exactly (review.rs, unblock.rs are the closest analogues)
Each task delivers working code + tests together
No schema migration — reuse existing resolve_decision/defer_decision DB functions where possible
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: StoredKeyDecision has resolution: Option<String> and resolved_at: Option<String> fields
Positive: map_row reads resolution (index 9) and resolved_at (index 10) from query results
Positive: get_pending_decisions and get_all_pending_decisions SELECT clauses include resolution, resolved_at
Positive: get_decision_by_id returns Ok(Some(decision)) for existing ID, Ok(None) for missing
Positive: get_all_decisions(conn, None) returns all decisions regardless of status
Positive: get_all_decisions(conn, Some('pending')) returns only pending decisions
Positive: revert_decision sets status='pending', resolution=NULL, resolved_at=NULL
Negative: revert_decision on already-pending decision returns InvalidState error
Negative: No changes to existing function signatures — engine.rs callers must compile without changes
Unit test: insert decision, resolve it, get_decision_by_id returns status='resolved' with resolution text
Unit test: insert decision, revert fails with InvalidState (already pending)
Unit test: insert + resolve + revert succeeds, status back to 'pending', resolution is None
Unit test: get_all_decisions returns resolved AND pending decisions
Known-bad: updating map_row without updating SELECT queries causes runtime panic — test all existing query functions still work after the change
Positive: error.rs has decision_not_found constructor following task_not_found pattern
userStories.description:
Extend the StoredKeyDecision struct with resolution/resolved_at fields, update all queries, and add new DB functions for the decisions management commands.

DO:
- Add `resolution: Option<String>` and `resolved_at: Option<String>` to StoredKeyDecision
- Update map_row to read 11 columns (currently reads 9, skipping resolution and resolved_at)
- Update ALL SELECT queries in this file to include `resolution, resolved_at` columns
- Add get_decision_by_id(conn, id) -> Result<Option<StoredKeyDecision>>
- Add get_all_decisions(conn, status_filter: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<StoredKeyDecision>>
- Add revert_decision(conn, id) that SETs status='pending', resolution=NULL, resolved_at=NULL
- Add a decision_not_found convenience constructor to error.rs (follow task_not_found pattern at line 215)

DO NOT:
- Change the signature of existing functions (resolve_decision, defer_decision, etc.) — callers in engine.rs depend on them
- Add a schema migration — the columns already exist in the DB, we're just reading them now
- Add 'approved' or 'declined' to any CHECK constraint

Edge cases:
- get_decision_by_id with non-existent ID returns Ok(None), not Err
- get_all_decisions with status_filter=None returns ALL decisions (pending, resolved, deferred)
- revert_decision on an already-pending decision should be an error (InvalidState)
- Existing tests must still pass after map_row changes (they don't assert on resolution/resolved_at columns, but the column indices shift)

Known-bad to guard against:
- If you update map_row to read 11 columns but forget to update the SELECT in get_pending_decisions or get_all_pending_decisions, you'll get runtime column-index-out-of-bounds errors. ALL SELECTs must be updated atomically.
userStories.notes:
Key files:
- src/db/schema/key_decisions.rs (main changes)
- src/error.rs (add decision_not_found at ~line 221)

Existing patterns to follow:
- task_not_found at error.rs:215 for the NotFound constructor
- The existing map_row function at key_decisions.rs:125 — extend it
- setup_db() test helper at key_decisions.rs:166 — reuse for new tests
- make_decision() test helper at key_decisions.rs:177 — reuse for new tests

The SELECT column order MUST be: id, run_id, task_id, iteration, title, description, options, status, created_at, resolution, resolved_at
userStories.title: Extend StoredKeyDecision and add DB functions

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `task-mgr decisions list` parses correctly via clap (cargo build succeeds)
Positive: `task-mgr decisions resolve 3 A` parses decision_id=3, option='A' as positional args
Positive: `task-mgr decisions decline 5 --reason 'Not MVP'` parses correctly
Positive: `task-mgr decisions revert 3` parses correctly
Positive: list_decisions returns pending/deferred by default, all with --all flag
Positive: resolve_decision_cmd with letter 'a' selects first option (case-insensitive)
Positive: resolve_decision_cmd with letter 'B' selects second option
Positive: decline_decision_cmd with no reason defaults to 'DECLINED: Not needed'
Positive: decline_decision_cmd with reason produces 'DECLINED: {reason}' resolution
Negative: resolve_decision_cmd on resolved decision returns InvalidState error
Negative: resolve_decision_cmd with invalid letter (e.g., 'Z' for 2-option decision) returns clear error
Negative: revert_decision_cmd on pending decision returns InvalidState error
Unit test: resolve with 'a' on 2-option decision picks first option
Unit test: resolve on resolved decision returns error
Unit test: decline defaults to 'DECLINED: Not needed'
Unit test: revert on resolved decision succeeds, on pending fails
Unit test: format_list_text with 0 decisions shows 'No pending key decisions'
Unit test: format_list_text with decisions shows ID, status, title, options count
Known-bad: if option parsing uses (ch as u8 - b'a') without bounds check, passing 'Z' causes out-of-bounds panic instead of error
userStories.description:
Add the Decisions variant to the Commands enum, the DecisionAction subcommand enum, and implement the full command module with result structs, logic functions, and format functions.

DO:
- Add Decisions { action: DecisionAction } variant to Commands enum in src/cli/commands.rs
- Add DecisionAction enum with List, Resolve, Decline, Revert subcommands (see plan for exact definitions)
- Add DecisionAction to re-exports in src/cli/mod.rs
- Create src/commands/decisions.rs with result structs, logic functions, format functions
- Add pub mod decisions + re-exports to src/commands/mod.rs
- Resolve uses positional args: decision_id and option (no --option flag)
- Decline uses positional decision_id + optional --reason flag

DO NOT:
- Create a separate enums entry for DecisionStatusFilter — use a simple --all bool flag instead
- Add the main.rs dispatch or handlers.rs registration (that's FEAT-003)
- Use unwrap() — map errors via TaskMgrError

Edge cases:
- resolve_decision_cmd: option_str 'a' and 'A' both map to first option (case-insensitive)
- resolve_decision_cmd: option_str 'Z' with only 2 options → clear error message
- resolve_decision_cmd: option_str that matches an option label (substring) also works
- decline_decision_cmd: no reason provided → default 'Not needed'
- revert_decision_cmd: decision already pending → InvalidState error
- list_decisions with --all=false (default) shows only pending/deferred

Known-bad to guard against:
- If option letter parsing uses 0-based without wrapping_sub(b'a'), index calculation is wrong
- If decline calls defer_decision instead of resolve_decision, status becomes 'deferred' not 'resolved' and the decision keeps appearing in pending lists
userStories.notes:
Key files:
- src/cli/commands.rs — add Decisions variant near line 888 (after Curate), add DecisionAction enum
- src/cli/mod.rs — add DecisionAction to the re-export line 23
- src/commands/decisions.rs — NEW FILE, follow review.rs pattern exactly
- src/commands/mod.rs — add pub mod decisions + re-exports

Patterns to follow:
- review.rs for result struct shape, format_text pattern, and test helpers
- RunAction/WorktreesAction in commands.rs for the subcommand enum pattern
- The option parsing should follow the session-end prompt pattern in engine.rs:1970-1987

Result structs must derive Serialize for JSON output. Use #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] on optional fields.
userStories.title: CLI subcommand definition + command implementation

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: cargo build succeeds with all wiring in place
Positive: task-mgr decisions list produces text output
Positive: task-mgr --format json decisions list produces JSON output
Positive: task-mgr decisions resolve/decline/revert acquire LockGuard before DB access
Positive: task-mgr decisions list does NOT acquire LockGuard
Negative: No unwrap() in the dispatch code — all errors propagated via ?
Known-bad: forgetting impl_text_formattable! causes runtime panic on text output — verify text output works for all 4 result types
userStories.description:
Connect the decisions command module to the CLI entry point and output system.

DO:
- Add Commands::Decisions { action } match arm to run() in main.rs
- Dispatch List → list_decisions (read-only, no LockGuard)
- Dispatch Resolve → resolve_decision_cmd (LockGuard + open_connection)
- Dispatch Decline → decline_decision_cmd (LockGuard + open_connection)
- Dispatch Revert → revert_decision_cmd (LockGuard + open_connection)
- Import DecisionAction from cli module
- Import decision functions from commands module
- Add impl_text_formattable! for DecisionsListResult, DecisionResolveResult, DecisionDeclineResult, DecisionRevertResult in handlers.rs
- Import decision format functions and result types in handlers.rs

DO NOT:
- Add verbose output — keep it simple for v1
- Add format_json override — the generic output_result handles JSON via Serialize

Edge cases:
- List is read-only (no lock needed)
- Resolve/Decline/Revert mutate the DB (need LockGuard)

Known-bad to guard against:
- If you forget to register impl_text_formattable!, text output will compile but panic at runtime
- If you import from wrong module path (e.g., commands::decisions vs db::schema::key_decisions), you'll get type mismatches
userStories.title: Wire dispatch in main.rs and register TextFormattable in handlers.rs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: .claude/commands/tm-decisions.md exists and is valid markdown
Positive: skill includes step-by-step instructions for Claude
Positive: skill handles the 'no pending decisions' case
Positive: skill shows option labels and descriptions before asking user to choose
Positive: skill includes usage examples at the top
Negative: skill does NOT directly query the database — uses task-mgr CLI only
userStories.description:
Create the Claude Code skill command that provides an interactive interface for managing key decisions.

DO:
- Create .claude/commands/tm-decisions.md
- Step 1: Run task-mgr --format json decisions list to get pending decisions
- Step 2: Present each decision with full detail (title, description, all options with labels)
- Step 3: For each decision, ask the user what they want to do (resolve/decline/skip)
- Step 4: Run the appropriate CLI command
- Include usage examples at the top
- Handle the case where no decisions are pending

DO NOT:
- Make the skill overly complex — keep it as instructions for Claude, not a script
- Duplicate the CLI help text — focus on the interactive flow
- Include implementation details about the DB — the skill operates through the CLI
userStories.notes:
Follow the pattern of existing skills like tm-status.md and tm-apply.md.

The skill should use --format json to get structured data, then present it in a readable format.

Key commands the skill will run:
- task-mgr --format json decisions list
- task-mgr decisions resolve <id> <option>
- task-mgr decisions decline <id> --reason '<reason>'
- task-mgr decisions revert <id>
userStories.title: Add /tm-decisions skill command

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: No function longer than 30 lines in new code

userStories.description:
Review all new code for opportunities to improve.

DO:
- Check for duplicated logic between decisions.rs and the session-end prompt code in engine.rs (option parsing, display formatting)
- Check function lengths — anything over 30 lines should be broken up
- Check that all public functions have doc comments
- Verify separation of concerns (DB layer vs command layer vs format layer)
- Look for opportunities to extract shared helpers

DO NOT:
- Refactor existing code outside the scope of this feature
- Add unnecessary abstractions for one-time operations
userStories.title: Review for DRY, testability, and code quality

Positive: All errors propagated with context (map_err, not unwrap)
Positive: cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check all clean
Positive: JSON output works for all 4 subcommands (list, resolve, decline, revert)
Positive: Existing key_decisions tests still pass (map_row changes didn't break them)
Positive: Session-end interactive prompt still works (engine.rs callers unchanged)
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths
Negative: No dead code warnings for new code
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON with priority 50-98
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring. Run full test suite.

DO: Check every new function for error handling, check callers still work, verify no dead code.
DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — unconnected code is the #1 failure mode.
userStories.notes: If issues found: add FIX-xxx tasks to JSON, commit JSON changes. Loop picks them up automatically. If no issues: mark passes:true with note 'Clean review'.
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
